summary: Release risk is low. The change hardens two gate-evidence primitives without altering output for real callers: safeReaddir now treats ENOTDIR like ENOENT (returns []) so corrupt/partial workspaces degrade gracefully instead of crashing the scan feeding pr prepare/merge/reconcile, while re-throwing all other errors; buildEvidenceItem reorders the spread so explicit kind/ref win, provably output-identical for every caller because defaulted fields already used extra.X ?? default, and the one caller relying on the old ordering was fixed in the same diff. Rollback is trivial (two small self-contained functions, no schema/data migration). Verified green with 53/53 tests across the requested suites plus the two fix-anchored story test files.

inspection.summary: Inspected git diff origin/main for both files; all 12 buildEvidenceItem call sites and 3 safeReaddir call sites via grep; buildVerificationEvidenceItem caller (9306) and updated classifySeniorAxisEvidence (8357); new test assertions. Ran node --test across 8 suites (53 pass / 0 fail).
inputs: all 12 buildEvidenceItem call sites + 3 safeReaddir call sites

judgment_delta: Initial: reordering a spread and swallowing another errno could silently change evidence payloads or hide failures across merge path. After tracing every caller and confirming the ?? default pattern makes spread order irrelevant for non-kind/ref keys, that ENOTDIR is the sibling of already-swallowed ENOENT with all other errors propagating, and fix-anchored tests pass 53/0 → pass: provably output-preserving and strictly more robust, trivial rollback.
